warning_count: usize,\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\timpl Default for AlertManager {\n262\t    fn default() -> Self {\n263\t        Self::new(100)\n264\t    }\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\timpl AlertManager {\n268\t    /// Create a new alert manager with a maximum number of active alerts.\n269\t    pub fn new(max_active: usize) -> Self {\n270\t        Self {\n271\t            alerts: HashMap::new(),\n272\t            active_keys: HashMap::new(),\n273\t            next_id: 1,\n274\t            max_active,\n275\t            critical_count: 0,\n276\t            warning_count: 0,\n277\t        }\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    /// Raise a new alert or increment occurrence if duplicate.\n281\t    pub fn raise(\n282\t        &mut self,\n283\t        alert_type: AlertType,\n284\t        worker_id: impl Into<String>,\n285\t        message: Option<String>,\n286\t    ) -> u64 {\n287\t        let worker_id = worker_id.into();\n288\t        let key = (worker_id.clone(), alert_type);\n289\t\n290\t        // Check for existing active alert\n291\t        if let Some(&existing_id) = self.active_keys.get(&key) {\n292\t            if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&existing_id) {\n293\t                if alert.is_active {\n294\t                    alert.increment_occurrence();\n295\t                    if let Some(msg) = message {\n296\t                        alert.message = msg;\n297\t                    }\n298\t                    return existing_id;\n299\t                }\n300\t            }\n301\t        }\n302\t\n303\t        // Create new alert\n304\t        let id = self.next_id;\n305\t        self.next_id += 1;\n306\t\n307\t        let mut alert = HealthAlert::new(id, alert_type, worker_id.clone());\n308\t        if let Some(msg) = message {\n309\t            alert = alert.with_message(msg);\n310\t        }\n311\t\n312\t        // Update counts\n313\t        if !alert.acknowledged {\n314\t            match alert.severity {\n315\t                AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count += 1,\n316\t                AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count += 1,\n317\t                AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n318\t            }\n319\t        }\n320\t\n321\t        // Store alert\n322\t        self.alerts.insert(id, alert);\n323\t        self.active_keys.insert(key, id);\n324\t\n325\t        // Prune if needed\n326\t        if self.alerts.len() > self.max_active {\n327\t            self.prune_oldest_resolved();\n328\t        }\n329\t\n330\t        id\n331\t    }\n332\t\n333\t    /// Acknowledge an alert by ID.\n334\t    pub fn acknowledge(&mut self, alert_id: u64) -> bool {\n335\t        if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&alert_id) {\n336\t            if !alert.acknowledged {\n337\t                alert.acknowledge();\n338\t                // Update counts\n339\t                match alert.severity {\n340\t                    AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n341\t                    AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n342\t                    AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n343\t                }\n344\t            }\n345\t            return true;\n346\t        }\n347\t        false\n348\t    }\n349\t\n350\t    /// Acknowledge all alerts for a worker.\n351\t    pub fn acknowledge_all_for_worker(&mut self, worker_id: &str) -> usize {\n352\t        let mut count = 0;\n353\t        for alert in self.alerts.values_mut() {\n354\t            if alert.worker_id == worker_id && !alert.acknowledged && alert.is_active {\n355\t                alert.acknowledge();\n356\t                count += 1;\n357\t                // Update counts\n358\t                match alert.severity {\n359\t                    AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n360\t                    AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n361\t                    AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n362\t                }\n363\t            }\n364\t        }\n365\t        count\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Acknowledge all active alerts.\n369\t    pub fn acknowledge_all(&mut self) -> usize {\n370\t        let mut count = 0;\n371\t        for alert in self.alerts.values_mut() {\n372\t            if !alert.acknowledged && alert.is_active {\n373\t                alert.acknowledge();\n374\t                count += 1;\n375\t            }\n376\t        }\n377\t        self.critical_count = 0;\n378\t        self.warning_count = 0;\n379\t        count\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Resolve (clear) an alert - called when worker recovers.\n383\t    pub fn resolve(&mut self, alert_id: u64) -> bool {\n384\t        if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&alert_id) {\n385\t            if alert.is_active {\n386\t                alert.resolve();\n387\t                // Remove from active keys\n388\t                let key = (alert.worker_id.clone(), alert.alert_type);\n389\t                self.active_keys.remove(&key);\n390\t                // Update counts\n391\t                if !alert.acknowledged {\n392\t                    match alert.severity {\n393\t                        AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n394\t                        AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n395\t                        AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n396\t                    }\n397\t                }\n398\t            }\n399\t            return true;\n400\t        }\n401\t        false\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    /// Resolve all alerts for a worker (called on recovery).\n405\t    pub fn resolve_all_for_worker(&mut self, worker_id: &str) -> usize {\n406\t        let mut count = 0;\n407\t        let ids: Vec<u64> = self\n408\t            .alerts\n409\t            .iter()\n410\t            .filter(|(_, a)| a.worker_id == worker_id && a.is_active)\n411\t            .map(|(id, _)| *id)\n412\t            .collect();\n413\t\n414\t        for id in ids {\n415\t            if self.resolve(id) {\n416\t                count += 1;\n417\t            }\n418\t        }\n419\t        count\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    /// Get an alert by ID.\n423\t    pub fn get(&self, alert_id: u64) -> Option<&HealthAlert> {\n424\t        self.alerts.get(&alert_id)\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    /// Get all active alerts (unacknowledged and acknowledged).\n428\t    pub fn active_alerts(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n429\t        self.alerts\n430\t            .values()\n431\t            .filter(|a| a.is_active)\n432\t            .collect()\n433\t    }\n434\t\n435\t    /// Get unacknowledged alerts only.\n436\t    pub fn unacknowledged_alerts(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n437\t        self.alerts\n438\t            .values()\n439\t            .filter(|a| a.is_active && !a.acknowledged)\n440\t            .collect()\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    /// Get alerts sorted by severity (critical first) and then by time.\n444\t    pub fn alerts_by_severity(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n445\t        let mut alerts: Vec<_> = self.active_alerts();\n446\t        alerts.sort_by(|a, b| {\n447\t            // Sort by: severity desc, then unacknowledged first, then time desc\n448\t            b.severity\n449\t                .cmp(&a.severity)\n450\t                .then(a.acknowledged.cmp(&b.acknowledged))\n451\t                .then(b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at))\n452\t        });\n453\t        alerts\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    /// Get count of active alerts.\n457\t    pub fn active_count(&self) -> usize {\n458\t        self.alerts.values().filter(|a| a.is_active).count()\n459\t    }\n460\t\n461\t    /// Get count of unacknowledged alerts.\n462\t    pub fn unacknowledged_count(&self) -> usize {\n463\t        self.alerts\n464\t            .values()\n465\t            .filter(|a| a.is_active && !a.acknowledged)\n466\t            .count()\n467\t    }\n468\t\n469\t    /// Get count of critical unacknowledged alerts.\n470\t    pub fn critical_count(&self) -> usize {\n471\t        self.critical_count\n472\t    }\n473\t\n474\t    /// Get count of warning unacknowledged alerts.\n475\t    pub fn warning_count(&self) -> usize {\n476\t        self.warning_count\n477\t    }\n478\t\n479\t    /// Check if there are any active alerts.\n480\t    pub fn has_alerts(&self) -> bool {\n481\t        self.alerts.values().any(|a| a.is_active)\n482\t    }\n483\t\n484\t    /// Check if there are any unacknowledged alerts.\n485\t    pub fn has_unacknowledged(&self) -> bool {\n486\t        self.critical_count > 0 || self.warning_count > 0\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    /// Get alert summary for badge display.\n490\t    pub fn badge_summary(&self) -> AlertBadge {\n491\t        AlertBadge {\n492\t            critical: self.critical_count,\n493\t            warning: self.warning_count,\n494\t            total: self.unacknowledged_count(),\n495\t        }\n496\t    }\n497\t\n498\t    /// Clear all alerts (for testing/reset).\n499\t    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n500\t        self.alerts.clear();\n501\t        self.active_keys.clear();\n502\t        self.critical_count = 0;\n503\t        self.warning_count = 0;\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    /// Prune oldest resolved alerts.\n507\t    fn prune_oldest_resolved(&mut self) {\n508\t        let mut resolved: Vec<_> = self\n509\t            .alerts\n510\t            .iter()\n511\t            .filter(|(_, a)| !a.is_active)\n512\t            .collect();\n513\t        resolved.sort_by_key(|(_, a)| a.created_at);\n514\t\n515\t        let to_remove: Vec<u64> = resolved\n516\t            .into_iter()\n517\t            .take(self.alerts.len().saturating_sub(self.max_active))\n518\t            .map(|(id, _)| *id)\n519\t            .collect();\n520\t\n521\t        for id in to_remove {\n522\t            self.alerts.remove(&id);\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t/// Summary badge for alert display.\n528\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]\n529\tpub struct AlertBadge {\n530\t    /// Critical alert count\n531\t    pub critical: usize,\n532\t    /// Warning alert count\n533\t    pub warning: usize,\n534\t    /// Total unacknowledged count\n535\t    pub total: usize,\n536\t}\n537\t\n538\timpl AlertBadge {\n539\t    /// Check if badge should be displayed.\n540\t    pub fn should_display(&self) -> bool {\n541\t        self.total > 0\n542\t    }\n543\t\n544\t    /// Format for display in header.\n545\t    pub fn format_header(&self) -> String {\n546\t        if self.critical > 0 {\n547\t            format!(\"⚠ {} critical | {} warning\", self.critical, self.warning)\n548\t        } else if self.warning > 0 {\n549\t            format!(\"⚠ {} warning\", self.warning)\n550\t        } else {\n551\t            String::new()\n552\t        }\n553\t    }\n554\t\n555\t    /// Format compact badge (just count and icon).\n556\t    pub fn format_compact(&self) -> String {\n557\t        if self.critical > 0 {\n558\t            format!(\"⚠️{}\", self.critical)\n559\t        } else if self.warning > 0 {\n560\t            format!(\"⚡{}\", self.warning)\n561\t        } else {\n562\t            String::new()\n563\t        }\n564\t    }\n565\t}\n566\t\n567\t/// Notification manager for alert sounds and visual feedback.\n568\t///\n569\t/// Handles terminal bell notifications and tracks cooldowns to avoid spam.\n570\t#[derive(Debug)]\n571\tpub struct AlertNotifier {\n572\t    /// Last time a bell was triggered\n573\t    last_bell: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n574\t    /// Minimum interval between bells (in seconds)\n575\t    bell_interval_secs: u64,\n576\t    /// Whether bell is enabled for critical alerts\n577\t    bell_on_critical: bool,\n578\t    /// Whether bell is enabled for warning alerts\n579\t    bell_on_warning: bool,\n580\t    /// Whether visual flash is enabled\n581\t    visual_flash_enabled: bool,\n582\t    /// Pending bell to be triggered on next render\n583\t    pending_bell: bool,\n584\t    /// Pending flash to be triggered on next render\n585\t    pending_flash: bool,\n586\t    /// Flash start time (for timing the flash duration)\n587\t    flash_start: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\timpl Default for AlertNotifier {\n591\t    fn default() -> Self {\n592\t        Self {\n593\t            last_bell: None,\n594\t            bell_interval_secs: 30,\n595\t            bell_on_critical: true,\n596\t            bell_on_warning: false,\n597\t            visual_flash_enabled: true,\n598\t            pending_bell: false,\n599\t            pending_flash: false,\n600\t            flash_start: None,\n601\t        }\n602\t    }\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\timpl AlertNotifier {\n606\t    /// Create a new alert notifier with default settings.\n607\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n608\t        Self::default()\n609\t    }\n610\t\n611\t    /// Configure the notifier from settings.\n612\t    pub fn configure(\n613\t        &mut self,\n614\t        bell_on_critical: bool,\n615\t        bell_on_warning: bool,\n616\t        bell_interval_secs: u64,\n617\t        visual_flash_enabled: bool,\n618\t    ) {\n619\t        self.bell_on_critical = bell_on_critical;\n620\t        self.bell_on_warning = bell_on_warning;\n621\t        self.bell_interval_secs = bell_interval_secs;\n622\t        self.visual_flash_enabled = visual_flash_enabled;\n623\t    }\n624\t\n625\t    /// Notify about a new alert (queues bell/flash if applicable).\n626\t    pub fn notify(&mut self, severity: AlertSeverity) {\n627\t        let should_bell = match severity {\n628\t            AlertSeverity::Critical => self.bell_on_critical,\n629\t            AlertSeverity::Warning => self.bell_on_warning,\n630\t            AlertSeverity::Info => false,\n631\t        };\n632\t\n633\t        if should_bell && self.can_ring_bell() {\n634\t            self.pending_bell = true;\n635\t        }\n636\t\n637\t        if self.visual_flash_enabled && severity == AlertSeverity::Critical {\n638\t            self.pending_flash = true;\n639\t            self.flash_start = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n640\t        }\n641\t    }\n642\t\n643\t    /// Check if enough time has passed to ring the bell again.\n644\t    fn can_ring_bell(&self) -> bool {\n645\t        match self.last_bell {\n646\t            None => true,\n647\t            Some(last) => {\n648\t                last.elapsed().as_secs() >= self.bell_interval_secs\n649\t            }\n650\t        }\n651\t    }\n652\t\n653\t    /// Take and clear the pending bell flag, returning whether to ring the bell.\n654\t    ///\n655\t    /// Call this during the render loop to trigger the bell.\n656\t    pub fn take_pending_bell(&mut self) -> bool {\n657\t        if self.pending_bell {\n658\t            self.pending_bell = false;\n659\t            self.last_bell = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n660\t            true\n661\t        } else {\n662\t            false\n663\t        }\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    /// Check if visual flash is currently active (within 200ms of alert).\n667\t    pub fn is_flashing(&self) -> bool {\n668\t        if let Some(start) = self.flash_start {\n669\t            start.elapsed().as_millis() < 200\n670\t        } else {\n671\t            false\n672\t        }\n673\t    }\n674\t\n675\t    /// Clear the flash state after it has been displayed.\n676\t    pub fn clear_flash(&mut self) {\n677\t        self.pending_flash = false;\n678\t        // Keep flash_start for timing\n679\t    }\n680\t\n681\t    /// Ring the terminal bell (BEL character).\n682\t    ///\n683\t    /// This outputs the ASCII BEL character (0x07) which causes most terminals\n684\t    /// to emit an audible beep or visual bell depending on terminal settings.\n685\t    pub fn ring_bell() {\n686\t        // Output BEL character (ASCII 7) to trigger terminal bell\n687\t        print!(\"\\x07\");\n688\t        // Flush to ensure it's sent immediately\n689\t        use std::io::Write;\n690\t        let _ = std::io::stdout().flush();\n691\t    }\n692\t}\n693\t\n694\t#[cfg(test)]\n695\tmod tests {\n696\t    use super::*;\n697\t\n698\t    #[test]\n699\t    fn test_alert_severity_ordering() {\n700\t        assert!(AlertSeverity::Critical > AlertSeverity::Warning);\n701\t        assert!(AlertSeverity::Warning > AlertSeverity::Info);\n702\t    }\n703\t\n704\t    #[test]\n705\t    fn test_alert_type_default_severity() {\n706\t        assert_eq!(\n707\t    }\n719\t\n720\t    #[test]\n721\t    fn test_health_alert_new() {\n722\t        let alert = HealthAlert::new(1, AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\");\n723\t        assert_eq!(alert.id, (200ms)\n956\t    }\n957\t}\n958\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b23055d8-7061-4f2c-acbf-2eba9b1d6ae0","uuid":"164e2613-0a0d-4d5e-bc03-a940ec9e157a","timestamp":"2026-05-04T10:56:52.168Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-tui/src/alert.rs","content":"//! Worker health alert system for FORGE.\n//!\n//! This module provides an alert system that tracks and displays notifications\n//! when workers become unresponsive, crash, or exhibit degraded health.\n//!\n//! ## Alert Types\n//!\n//! - **Critical**: Worker crashed (process died, PID missing)\n//! - **Warning**: Worker degraded (stale activity, stuck task)\n//! - **Info**: Recovery events, auto-restart triggered\n//!\n//! ## Features\n//!\n//! - Alert badge count in header\n//! - Alert summary in Overview panel\n//! - Acknowledgment/dismiss system\n//! - Auto-clear when worker recovers\n//! - Sound notification (future)\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Alert severity level.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum AlertSeverity {\n    /// Informational (recovery, auto-restart)\n    Info = 0,\n    /// Warning (degraded performance, stale activity)\n    Warning = 1,\n    /// Critical (crashed, dead process)\n    Critical = 2,\n}\n\nimpl AlertSeverity {\n    /// Get the icon for this severity level.\n    pub fn icon(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            AlertSeverity::Info => \"ℹ\",\n            AlertSeverity::Warning => \"⚠\",\n            AlertSeverity::Critical => \"✖\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this severity should trigger a notification.\n    pub fn should_notify(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AlertSeverity::Critical | AlertSeverity::Warning)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Types of worker health alerts.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum AlertType {\n    /// Worker process has died (PID check failed)\n    WorkerCrashed,\n    /// Worker process is a zombie\n    WorkerZombie,\n    /// Worker has no activity for too long\n    WorkerStale,\n    /// Worker task has been stuck for too long\n    TaskStuck,\n    /// Worker memory usage is too high\n    MemoryHigh,\n    /// Worker not responding to health checks\n    WorkerUnresponsive,\n    /// Auto-restart triggered\n    AutoRestartTriggered,\n    /// Recovery attempts exhausted\n    RecoveryExhausted,\n    /// Worker recovered (info only)\n    WorkerRecovered,\n    /// New worker spawned (info only)\n    WorkerSpawned,\n}\n\nimpl AlertType {\n    /// Get the default severity for this alert type.\n    pub fn default_severity(&self) -> AlertSeverity {\n        match self {\n            AlertType::WorkerCrashed\n            | AlertType::WorkerZombie\n            | AlertType::RecoveryExhausted => AlertSeverity::Critical,\n            AlertType::WorkerStale\n            | AlertType::TaskStuck\n            | AlertType::WorkerUnresponsive\n            | AlertType::AutoRestartTriggered => AlertSeverity::Warning,\n            AlertType::MemoryHigh => AlertSeverity::Warning,\n            AlertType::WorkerRecovered | AlertType::WorkerSpawned => AlertSeverity::Info,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a human-readable title for this alert type.\n    pub fn title(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            AlertType::WorkerCrashed => \"Worker Crashed\",\n            AlertType::WorkerZombie => \"Worker Zombie Process\",\n            AlertType::WorkerStale => \"Worker Stale Activity\",\n            AlertType::TaskStuck => \"Task Stuck\",\n            AlertType::MemoryHigh => \"Memory Usage High\",\n            AlertType::WorkerUnresponsive => \"Worker Unresponsive\",\n            AlertType::AutoRestartTriggered => \"Auto-Restart Triggered\",\n            AlertType::RecoveryExhausted => \"Recovery Exhausted\",\n            AlertType::WorkerRecovered => \"Worker Recovered\",\n            AlertType::WorkerSpawned => \"Worker Spawned\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the default message for this alert type.\n    pub fn default_message(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            AlertType::WorkerCrashed => \"Worker process has terminated unexpectedly\",\n            AlertType::WorkerZombie => \"Worker process is in zombie state\",\n            AlertType::WorkerStale => \"No activity detected from worker\",\n            AlertType::TaskStuck => \"Current task has been running too long\",\n            AlertType::MemoryHigh => \"Memory usage exceeds threshold\",\n            AlertType::WorkerUnresponsive => \"Worker not responding to health checks\",\n            AlertType::AutoRestartTriggered => \"Worker will be restarted automatically\",\n            AlertType::RecoveryExhausted => \"Max recovery attempts reached\",\n            AlertType::WorkerRecovered => \"Worker health restored\",\n            AlertType::WorkerSpawned => \"New worker started\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single health alert.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct HealthAlert {\n    /// Unique alert identifier\n    pub id: u64,\n    /// Type of alert\n    pub alert_type: AlertType,\n    /// Severity level\n    pub severity: AlertSeverity,\n    /// Worker ID this alert is for\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    /// Alert title\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Detailed message\n    pub message: String,\n    /// When the alert was first raised\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// When the alert was last updated\n    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Whether the alert has been acknowledged\n    pub acknowledged: bool,\n    /// Number of times this alert has occurred\n    pub occurrence_count: u32,\n    /// Whether this is an active alert (vs historical)\n    pub is_active: bool,\n}\n\nimpl HealthAlert {\n    /// Create a new alert.\n    pub fn new(\n        id: u64,\n        alert_type: AlertType,\n        worker_id: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            alert_type,\n            severity: alert_type.default_severity(),\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n            title: alert_type.title().to_string(),\n            message: alert_type.default_message().to_string(),\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            acknowledged: false,\n            occurrence_count: 1,\n            is_active: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create with a custom message.\n    pub fn with_message(mut self, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.message = message.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Create with a custom severity.\n    pub fn with_severity(mut self, severity: AlertSeverity) -> Self {\n        self.severity = severity;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge this alert.\n    pub fn acknowledge(&mut self) {\n        self.acknowledged = true;\n        self.updated_at = Utc::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Mark this alert as resolved/inactive.\n    pub fn resolve(&mut self) {\n        self.is_active = false;\n        self.updated_at = Utc::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment occurrence count (for deduplication).\n    pub fn increment_occurrence(&mut self) {\n        self.occurrence_count += 1;\n        self.updated_at = Utc::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Format for display in one line.\n    pub fn format_compact(&self) -> String {\n        let ack_marker = if self.acknowledged { \"✓\" } else { \" \" };\n        let count_marker = if self.occurrence_count > 1 {\n            format!(\" (x{})\", self.occurrence_count)\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        };\n        format!(\n            \"{} {} [{}] {}{}\",\n            ack_marker,\n            self.severity.icon(),\n            self.worker_id,\n            self.title,\n            count_marker\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Format for detailed display.\n    pub fn format_detail(&self) -> String {\n        let time = self.created_at.format(\"%H:%M:%S\");\n        let ack_status = if self.acknowledged { \"acknowledged\" } else { \"active\" };\n        let count_info = if self.occurrence_count > 1 {\n            format!(\" (occurred {} times)\", self.occurrence_count)\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        };\n\n        format!(\n            \"[{}] {} [{}] {} - {}{}\\n  {}\",\n            time,\n            self.severity.icon(),\n            ack_status,\n            self.worker_id,\n            self.title,\n            count_info,\n            self.message\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Alert manager that tracks and manages health alerts.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct AlertManager {\n    /// All alerts by ID\n    alerts: HashMap<u64, HealthAlert>,\n    /// Active alerts by (worker_id, alert_type) for deduplication\n    active_keys: HashMap<(String, AlertType), u64>,\n    /// Next alert ID\n    next_id: u64,\n    /// Maximum active alerts to track\n    max_active: usize,\n    /// Count of unacknowledged critical alerts\n    critical_count: usize,\n    /// Count of unacknowledged warning alerts\n    warning_count: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AlertManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(100)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AlertManager {\n    /// Create a new alert manager with a maximum number of active alerts.\n    pub fn new(max_active: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            alerts: HashMap::new(),\n            active_keys: HashMap::new(),\n            next_id: 1,\n            max_active,\n            critical_count: 0,\n            warning_count: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Raise a new alert or increment occurrence if duplicate.\n    pub fn raise(\n        &mut self,\n        alert_type: AlertType,\n        worker_id: impl Into<String>,\n        message: Option<String>,\n    ) -> u64 {\n        let worker_id = worker_id.into();\n        let key = (worker_id.clone(), alert_type);\n\n        // Check for existing active alert\n        if let Some(&existing_id) = self.active_keys.get(&key) {\n            if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&existing_id) {\n                if alert.is_active {\n                    alert.increment_occurrence();\n                    if let Some(msg) = message {\n                        alert.message = msg;\n                    }\n                    return existing_id;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Create new alert\n        let id = self.next_id;\n        self.next_id += 1;\n\n        let mut alert = HealthAlert::new(id, alert_type, worker_id.clone());\n        if let Some(msg) = message {\n            alert = alert.with_message(msg);\n        }\n\n        // Update counts\n        if !alert.acknowledged {\n            match alert.severity {\n                AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count += 1,\n                AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count += 1,\n                AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Store alert\n        self.alerts.insert(id, alert);\n        self.active_keys.insert(key, id);\n\n        // Prune if needed\n        if self.alerts.len() > self.max_active {\n            self.prune_oldest_resolved();\n        }\n\n        id\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge an alert by ID.\n    pub fn acknowledge(&mut self, alert_id: u64) -> bool {\n        if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&alert_id) {\n            if !alert.acknowledged {\n                alert.acknowledge();\n                // Update counts\n                match alert.severity {\n                    AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                    AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                    AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n                }\n            }\n            return true;\n        }\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge all alerts for a worker.\n    pub fn acknowledge_all_for_worker(&mut self, worker_id: &str) -> usize {\n        let mut count = 0;\n        for alert in self.alerts.values_mut() {\n            if alert.worker_id == worker_id && !alert.acknowledged && alert.is_active {\n                alert.acknowledge();\n                count += 1;\n                // Update counts\n                match alert.severity {\n                    AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                    AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                    AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        count\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge all active alerts.\n    pub fn acknowledge_all(&mut self) -> usize {\n        let mut count = 0;\n        for alert in self.alerts.values_mut() {\n            if !alert.acknowledged && alert.is_active {\n                alert.acknowledge();\n                count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        self.critical_count = 0;\n        self.warning_count = 0;\n        count\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve (clear) an alert - called when worker recovers.\n    pub fn resolve(&mut self, alert_id: u64) -> bool {\n        if let Some(alert) = self.alerts.get_mut(&alert_id) {\n            if alert.is_active {\n                alert.resolve();\n                // Remove from active keys\n                let key = (alert.worker_id.clone(), alert.alert_type);\n                self.active_keys.remove(&key);\n                // Update counts\n                if !alert.acknowledged {\n                    match alert.severity {\n                        AlertSeverity::Critical => self.critical_count = self.critical_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                        AlertSeverity::Warning => self.warning_count = self.warning_count.saturating_sub(1),\n                        AlertSeverity::Info => {}\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            return true;\n        }\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve all alerts for a worker (called on recovery).\n    pub fn resolve_all_for_worker(&mut self, worker_id: &str) -> usize {\n        let mut count = 0;\n        let ids: Vec<u64> = self\n            .alerts\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|(_, a)| a.worker_id == worker_id && a.is_active)\n            .map(|(id, _)| *id)\n            .collect();\n\n        for id in ids {\n            if self.resolve(id) {\n                count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        count\n    }\n\n    /// Get an alert by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, alert_id: u64) -> Option<&HealthAlert> {\n        self.alerts.get(&alert_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all active alerts (unacknowledged and acknowledged).\n    pub fn active_alerts(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n        self.alerts\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.is_active)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get unacknowledged alerts only.\n    pub fn unacknowledged_alerts(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n        self.alerts\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.is_active && !a.acknowledged)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get alerts sorted by severity (critical first) and then by time.\n    pub fn alerts_by_severity(&self) -> Vec<&HealthAlert> {\n        let mut alerts: Vec<_> = self.active_alerts();\n        alerts.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            // Sort by: severity desc, then unacknowledged first, then time desc\n            b.severity\n                .cmp(&a.severity)\n                .then(a.acknowledged.cmp(&b.acknowledged))\n                .then(b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at))\n        });\n        alerts\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of active alerts.\n    pub fn active_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.alerts.values().filter(|a| a.is_active).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of unacknowledged alerts.\n    pub fn unacknowledged_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.alerts\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.is_active && !a.acknowledged)\n            .count()\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of critical unacknowledged alerts.\n    pub fn critical_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.critical_count\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of warning unacknowledged alerts.\n    pub fn warning_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.warning_count\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any active alerts.\n    pub fn has_alerts(&self) -> bool {\n        self.alerts.values().any(|a| a.is_active)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any unacknowledged alerts.\n    pub fn has_unacknowledged(&self) -> bool {\n        self.critical_count > 0 || self.warning_count > 0\n    }\n\n    /// Get alert summary for badge display.\n    pub fn badge_summary(&self) -> AlertBadge {\n        AlertBadge {\n            critical: self.critical_count,\n            warning: self.warning_count,\n            total: self.unacknowledged_count(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all alerts (for testing/reset).\n    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n        self.alerts.clear();\n        self.active_keys.clear();\n        self.critical_count = 0;\n        self.warning_count = 0;\n    }\n\n    /// Prune oldest resolved alerts.\n    fn prune_oldest_resolved(&mut self) {\n        let mut resolved: Vec<_> = self\n            .alerts\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|(_, a)| !a.is_active)\n            .collect();\n        resolved.sort_by_key(|(_, a)| a.created_at);\n\n        let to_remove: Vec<u64> = resolved\n            .into_iter()\n            .take(self.alerts.len().saturating_sub(self.max_active))\n            .map(|(id, _)| *id)\n            .collect();\n\n        for id in to_remove {\n            self.alerts.remove(&id);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Summary badge for alert display.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]\npub struct AlertBadge {\n    /// Critical alert count\n    pub critical: usize,\n    /// Warning alert count\n    pub warning: usize,\n    /// Total unacknowledged count\n    pub total: usize,\n}\n\nimpl AlertBadge {\n    /// Check if badge should be displayed.\n    pub fn should_display(&self) -> bool {\n        self.total > 0\n    }\n\n    /// Format for display in header.\n    pub fn format_header(&self) -> String {\n        if self.critical > 0 {\n            format!(\"⚠ {} critical | {} warning\", self.critical, self.warning)\n        } else if self.warning > 0 {\n            format!(\"⚠ {} warning\", self.warning)\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Format compact badge (just count and icon).\n    pub fn format_compact(&self) -> String {\n        if self.critical > 0 {\n            format!(\"⚠️{}\", self.critical)\n        } else if self.warning > 0 {\n            format!(\"⚡{}\", self.warning)\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Notification manager for alert sounds and visual feedback.\n///\n/// Handles terminal bell notifications and tracks cooldowns to avoid spam.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct AlertNotifier {\n    /// Last time a bell was triggered\n    last_bell: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Minimum interval between bells (in seconds)\n    bell_interval_secs: u64,\n    /// Whether bell is enabled for critical alerts\n    bell_on_critical: bool,\n    /// Whether bell is enabled for warning alerts\n    bell_on_warning: bool,\n    /// Whether visual flash is enabled\n    visual_flash_enabled: bool,\n    /// Pending bell to be triggered on next render\n    pending_bell: bool,\n    /// Pending flash to be triggered on next render\n    pending_flash: bool,\n    /// Flash start time (for timing the flash duration)\n    flash_start: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AlertNotifier {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            last_bell: None,\n            bell_interval_secs: 30,\n            bell_on_critical: true,\n            bell_on_warning: false,\n            visual_flash_enabled: true,\n            pending_bell: false,\n            pending_flash: false,\n            flash_start: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AlertNotifier {\n    /// Create a new alert notifier with default settings.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Configure the notifier from settings.\n    pub fn configure(\n        &mut self,\n        bell_on_critical: bool,\n        bell_on_warning: bool,\n        bell_interval_secs: u64,\n        visual_flash_enabled: bool,\n    ) {\n        self.bell_on_critical = bell_on_critical;\n        self.bell_on_warning = bell_on_warning;\n        self.bell_interval_secs = bell_interval_secs;\n        self.visual_flash_enabled = visual_flash_enabled;\n    }\n\n    /// Notify about a new alert (queues bell/flash if applicable).\n    pub fn notify(&mut self, severity: AlertSeverity) {\n        let should_bell = match severity {\n            AlertSeverity::Critical => self.bell_on_critical,\n            AlertSeverity::Warning => self.bell_on_warning,\n            AlertSeverity::Info => false,\n        };\n\n        if should_bell && self.can_ring_bell() {\n            self.pending_bell = true;\n        }\n\n        if self.visual_flash_enabled && severity == AlertSeverity::Critical {\n            self.pending_flash = true;\n            self.flash_start = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if enough time has passed to ring the bell again.\n    fn can_ring_bell(&self) -> bool {\n        match self.last_bell {\n            None => true,\n            Some(last) => {\n                last.elapsed().as_secs() >= self.bell_interval_secs\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Take and clear the pending bell flag, returning whether to ring the bell.\n    ///\n    /// Call this during the render loop to trigger the bell.\n    pub fn take_pending_bell(&mut self) -> bool {\n        if self.pending_bell {\n            self.pending_bell = false;\n            self.last_bell = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n            true\n        } else {\n            false\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if visual flash is currently active (within 200ms of alert).\n    pub fn is_flashing(&self) -> bool {\n        if let Some(start) = self.flash_start {\n            start.elapsed().as_millis() < 200\n        } else {\n            false\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clear the flash state after it has been displayed.\n    pub fn clear_flash(&mut self) {\n        self.pending_flash = false;\n        // Keep flash_start for timing\n    }\n\n    /// Ring the terminal bell (BEL character).\n    ///\n    /// This outputs the ASCII BEL character (0x07) which causes most terminals\n    /// to emit an audible beep or visual bell depending on terminal settings.\n    pub fn ring_bell() {\n        // Output BEL character (ASCII 7) to trigger terminal bell\n        print!(\"\\x07\");\n        // Flush to ensure it's sent immediately\n        use std::io::Write;\n        let _ = std::io::stdout().flush();\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_severity_ordering() {\n        assert!(AlertSeverity::Critical > AlertSeverity::Warning);\n        assert!(AlertSeverity::Warning > AlertSeverity::Info);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_type_default_severity() {\n        assert_eq!(\n            AlertType::WorkerCrashed.default_severity(),\n            AlertSeverity::Critical\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            AlertType::WorkerStale.default_severity(),\n            AlertSeverity::Warning\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            AlertType::WorkerRecovered.default_severity(),\n            AlertSeverity::Info\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_health_alert_new() {\n        let alert = HealthAlert::new(1, AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\");\n        assert_eq!(alert.id, 1);\n        assert_eq!(alert.worker_id, \"worker-1\");\n        assert_eq!(alert.severity, AlertSeverity::Critical);\n        assert!(!alert.acknowledged);\n        assert!(alert.is_active);\n        assert_eq!(alert.occurrence_count, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_health_alert_with_message() {\n        let alert = HealthAlert::new(1, AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\")\n            .with_message(\"Process 12345 not found\");\n        assert_eq!(alert.message, \"Process 12345 not found\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_health_alert_acknowledge() {\n        let mut alert = HealthAlert::new(1, AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\");\n        assert!(!alert.acknowledged);\n        alert.acknowledge();\n        assert!(alert.acknowledged);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_new() {\n        let manager = AlertManager::new(50);\n        assert_eq!(manager.active_count(), 0);\n        assert!(!manager.has_alerts());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_raise() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        let id = manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\", None);\n        assert_eq!(id, 1);\n        assert_eq!(manager.active_count(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(manager.critical_count(), 1);\n        assert!(manager.has_alerts());\n        assert!(manager.has_unacknowledged());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_raise_duplicate() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        let id1 = manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerStale, \"worker-1\", None);\n        let id2 = manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerStale, \"worker-1\", None);\n\n        // Should return same ID for duplicate\n        assert_eq!(id1, id2);\n        assert_eq!(manager.active_count(), 1);\n\n        // Occurrence count should be incremented\n        let alert = manager.get(id1).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(alert.occurrence_count, 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_acknowledge() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\", None);\n        assert_eq!(manager.critical_count(), 1);\n\n        manager.acknowledge(1);\n        assert_eq!(manager.critical_count(), 0);\n        assert!(manager.get(1).unwrap().acknowledged);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_resolve() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\", None);\n        assert!(manager.has_alerts());\n\n        manager.resolve(1);\n        assert!(!manager.has_alerts());\n        assert!(!manager.get(1).unwrap().is_active);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_manager_resolve_all_for_worker() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\", None);\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerStale, \"worker-1\", None);\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-2\", None);\n\n        assert_eq!(manager.active_count(), 3);\n\n        let resolved = manager.resolve_all_for_worker(\"worker-1\");\n        assert_eq!(resolved, 2);\n        assert_eq!(manager.active_count(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_badge() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        // No alerts\n        let badge = manager.badge_summary();\n        assert!(!badge.should_display());\n\n        // Add warning\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerStale, \"worker-1\", None);\n        let badge = manager.badge_summary();\n        assert!(badge.should_display());\n        assert_eq!(badge.warning, 1);\n        assert_eq!(badge.critical, 0);\n        assert_eq!(badge.total, 1);\n\n        // Add critical\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-2\", None);\n        let badge = manager.badge_summary();\n        assert_eq!(badge.critical, 1);\n        assert_eq!(badge.warning, 1);\n        assert_eq!(badge.total, 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alerts_by_severity() {\n        let mut manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerStale, \"worker-1\", None); // Warning\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-2\", None); // Critical\n        manager.raise(AlertType::WorkerRecovered, \"worker-3\", None); // Info\n\n        let alerts = manager.alerts_by_severity();\n        assert_eq!(alerts.len(), 3);\n        // Critical should be first\n        assert_eq!(alerts[0].alert_type, AlertType::WorkerCrashed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_format_compact() {\n        let alert = HealthAlert::new(1, AlertType::WorkerCrashed, \"worker-1\");\n        let compact = alert.format_compact();\n        assert!(compact.contains(\"✖\"));\n        assert!(compact.contains(\"worker-1\"));\n        assert!(compact.contains(\"Worker Crashed\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_new() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        // Default: bell on critical enabled, bell on warning disabled\n        assert!(!notifier.take_pending_bell());\n        assert!(!notifier.is_flashing());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_notify_critical() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        // Configure to enable bell on critical\n        notifier.configure(true, false, 30, true);\n\n        // Notify critical alert\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Critical);\n\n        // Should have pending bell\n        assert!(notifier.take_pending_bell());\n        // Should be flashing\n        assert!(notifier.is_flashing());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_notify_warning_disabled() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        // Configure: bell on critical only, warning disabled\n        notifier.configure(true, false, 30, false);\n\n        // Notify warning alert\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Warning);\n\n        // Should NOT have pending bell (warning disabled)\n        assert!(!notifier.take_pending_bell());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_notify_warning_enabled() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        // Configure: bell on warning enabled\n        notifier.configure(false, true, 30, false);\n\n        // Notify warning alert\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Warning);\n\n        // Should have pending bell\n        assert!(notifier.take_pending_bell());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_bell_interval() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        // Configure with 60 second interval\n        notifier.configure(true, false, 60, false);\n\n        // First notification - should ring\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Critical);\n        assert!(notifier.take_pending_bell());\n\n        // Second notification immediately after - should NOT ring (within interval)\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Critical);\n        assert!(!notifier.take_pending_bell());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_info_does_not_bell() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        notifier.configure(true, true, 30, true);\n\n        // Notify info alert\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Info);\n\n        // Should NOT have pending bell (info never bells)\n        assert!(!notifier.take_pending_bell());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alert_notifier_clear_flash() {\n        let mut notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n        notifier.configure(true, false, 30, true);\n\n        // Trigger flash\n        notifier.notify(AlertSeverity::Critical);\n        assert!(notifier.is_flashing());\n\n        // Clear flash\n        notifier.clear_flash();\n        // Flash is still \"active\" based on time, not pending_flash flag\n        // The is_flashing() check is time-based (200ms)\n    }\n}\n","numLines":958,"startLine":1,"totalLines":958}}}

Test all view hotkeys: w, t, c, m, l, o, :\n# 2. Verify each view renders correctly\n# 3. Test Tab/Shift+Tab navigation\n# 4. Validate help panel (? key)\n#\n# Exit codes:\n#   0 - Test passed\n#   1 - Test failed\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# Get script directory and source helpers\nSCRIPT_DIR=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"\nsource \"$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/test-helpers.sh\"\n\n# ==============================================================================\n# Test Configuration\n# ==============================================================================\n\nTEST_NAME=\"forge-views\"\n\n# View definitions with expected content\n# Format: \"key:view_name:expected_text\"\n# NOTE: lowercase 'o' is spawn Opus worker, NOT Overview!\n# Overview can only be accessed via 'O' (uppercase)\ndeclare -A VIEW_TESTS=(\n    [\"O\"]=\"Overview:Worker Pool\"\n    [\"w\"]=\"Workers:Worker Pool\"\n    [\"W\"]=\"Workers:Worker Pool\"\n    [\"t\"]=\"Tasks:Task Queue\"\n    [\"T\"]=\"Tasks:Task Queue\"\n    [\"c\"]=\"Costs:Cost\"\n    [\"m\"]=\"Metrics:Metrics\"\n    [\"l\"]=\"Logs:Activity Log\"\n    [\"a\"]=\"Logs:Activity Log\"\n)\n\n# ==============================================================================\n# Test Implementation\n# ==============================================================================\n\ntest_view_hotkeys() {\n    local session\n    session=$(get_session_name)\n    local result=0\n    local passed=0\n    local failed=0\n\n    log_info \"=== Testing View Hotkey Navigation ===\"\n\n    # Step 1: Start forge\n    log_info \"Step 1: Starting forge...\"\n    if ! start_forge \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Failed to start forge\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 2: Wait for initialization\n    log_info \"Step 2: Waiting for initialization...\"\n    if ! wait_for_init \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Forge failed to initialize\"\n        stop_forge \"$session\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 3: Test each view hotkey\n    log_info \"Step 3: Testing view hotkeys...\"\n\n    for key in \"${!VIEW_TESTS[@]}\"; do\n        local view_info=\"${VIEW_TESTS[$key]}\"\n        local view_name=\"${view_info%%:*}\"\n        local expected_text=\"${view_info#*:}\"\n\n        log_info \"Testing '$key' key for $view_name view...\"\n        send_key_wait \"$session\" \"$key\" 1\n\n        if pane_contains \"$session\" \"$expected_text\"; then\n            log_success \"'$key' -> $view_name: Found '$expected_text'\"\n            ((passed++))\n        else\n            log_fail \"'$key' -> $view_name: Expected '$expected_text' not found\"\n            ((failed++))\n            result=1\n        fi\n    done\n\n    # Step 4: Test Chat mode activation\n    log_info \"Step 4: Testing ':' for Chat mode...\"\n    send_key_wait \"$session\" \":\" 1\n\n    if pane_contains \"$session\" \"Chat\" || pane_contains \"$session\" \"Input\"; then\n        log_success \"':' -> Chat: Found chat interface\"\n        ((passed++))\n    else\n        log_fail \"':' -> Chat: Chat interface not found\"\n        ((failed++))\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Exit chat mode\n    send_keys \"$session\" \"Escape\"\n    sleep 0.5\n\n    log_info \"View hotkey tests: $passed passed, $failed failed\"\n\n    # Step 5: Cleanup\n    log_info \"Step 5: Cleaning up...\"\n    stop_forge \"$session\"\n\n    return $result\n}\n\ntest_tab_navigation() {\n    local session\n    session=$(get_session_name)\n    local result=0\n\n    log_info \"=== Testing Tab Navigation ===\"\n\n    # Step 1: Start forge\n    log_info \"Step 1: Starting forge...\"\n    if ! start_forge \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Failed to start forge\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 2: Wait for initialization\n    log_info \"Step 2: Waiting for initialization...\"\n    if ! wait_for_init \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Forge failed to initialize\"\n        stop_forge \"$session\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Starting view should be Overview\n    log_info \"Step 3: Verifying initial Overview view...\"\n    if ! pane_contains \"$session\" \"FORGE\"; then\n        log_fail \"Initial view should show FORGE header\"\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 4: Test Tab to cycle forward\n    log_info \"Step 4: Testing Tab key to cycle views...\"\n\n    # Views cycle: Overview -> Workers -> Tasks -> Costs -> Metrics -> Logs -> Chat -> Overview\n    local views=(\"Workers\" \"Tasks\" \"Costs\" \"Metrics\" \"Logs\" \"Chat\")\n    local tab_passed=0\n\n    for expected in \"${views[@]}\"; do\n        send_keys \"$session\" \"Tab\"\n        sleep 0.5\n\n        # Each view should have some identifying content\n        local found=false\n        case \"$expected\" in\n            \"Workers\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Worker\" && found=true ;;\n            \"Tasks\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Task\" && found=true ;;\n            \"Costs\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Cost\" && found=true ;;\n            \"Metrics\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Metric\" && found=true ;;\n            \"Logs\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Activity\" && found=true ;;\n            \"Chat\") pane_contains \"$session\" \"Chat\" && found=true ;;\n        esac\n\n        if $found; then\n            log_success \"Tab -> $expected: View found\"\n            ((tab_passed++))\n        else\n            log_warn \"Tab -> $expected: View content not verified\"\n        fi\n    done\n\n    log_info \"Tab navigation verified $tab_passed/${#views[@]} views\"\n\n    # Step 5: Test Shift+Tab to cycle backward\n    log_info \"Step 5: Testing Shift+Tab key for reverse cycling...\"\n\n    # From Chat, Shift+Tab should go to Logs\n    send_keys \"$session\" \"BTab\"  # BTab is Shift+Tab in tmux\n    sleep 0.5\n\n    if pane_contains \"$session\" \"Activity\" || pane_contains \"$session\" \"Log\"; then\n        log_success \"Shift+Tab reversed to Logs view\"\n    else\n        log_warn \"Could not verify Shift+Tab reverse navigation\"\n    fi\n\n    # Step 6: Cleanup\n    log_info \"Step 6: Cleaning up...\"\n    stop_forge \"$session\"\n\n    return $result\n}\n\ntest_help_overlay() {\n    local session\n    session=$(get_session_name)\n    local result=0\n\n    log_info \"=== Testing Help Overlay ===\"\n\n    # Step 1: Start forge\n    log_info \"Step 1: Starting forge...\"\n    if ! start_forge \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Failed to start forge\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 2: Wait for initialization\n    log_info \"Step 2: Waiting for initialization...\"\n    if ! wait_for_init \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Forge failed to initialize\"\n        stop_forge \"$session\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 3: Open help with '?'\n    log_info \"Step 3: Opening help overlay with '?' key...\"\n    send_key_wait \"$session\" \"?\" 1\n\n    # Check for help content\n    if assert_pane_contains \"$session\" \"Help\" \"Help overlay should be visible\"; then\n        log_success \"Help overlay opened\"\n    else\n        log_fail \"Help overlay not visible\"\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Check for hotkey documentation\n    if pane_contains \"$session\" \"Tab\" || pane_contains \"$session\" \"Navigation\"; then\n        log_success \"Help shows navigation information\"\n    else\n        log_warn \"Navigation info not visible in help\"\n    fi\n\n    if pane_contains \"$session\" \"Quit\" || pane_contains \"$session\" \"Esc\"; then\n        log_success \"Help shows quit/escape information\"\n    else\n        log_warn \"Quit/escape info not visible in help\"\n    fi\n\n    # Step 4: Close help with any key\n    log_info \"Step 4: Closing help overlay...\"\n    send_key_wait \"$session\" \"Escape\" 1\n\n    # Help should be closed - Overview content should be visible\n    if pane_contains \"$session\" \"Worker Pool\"; then\n        log_success \"Help overlay closed, main view visible\"\n    else\n        log_warn \"Could not verify help overlay closed\"\n    fi\n\n    # Step 5: Cleanup\n    log_info \"Step 5: Cleaning up...\"\n    stop_forge \"$session\"\n\n    return $result\n}\n\ntest_footer_hotkeys() {\n    local session\n    session=$(get_session_name)\n    local result=0\n\n    log_info \"=== Testing Footer Hotkey Hints ===\"\n\n    # Step 1: Start forge\n    log_info \"Step 1: Starting forge...\"\n    if ! start_forge \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Failed to start forge\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 2: Wait for initialization\n    log_info \"Step 2: Waiting for initialization...\"\n    if ! wait_for_init \"$session\"; then\n        log_fail \"Forge failed to initialize\"\n        stop_forge \"$session\"\n        return 1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 3: Check for footer hotkey hints\n    log_info \"Step 3: Verifying footer hotkey hints...\"\n\n    local hints=(\"[o]\" \"[w]\" \"[t]\" \"[c]\" \"[m]\" \"[l]\" \"[?]\" \"[q]\")\n    local hints_found=0\n\n    for hint in \"${hints[@]}\"; do\n        if pane_contains \"$session\" \"$hint\"; then\n            log_success \"Footer shows hint: $hint\"\n            ((hints_found++))\n        else\n            log_warn \"Footer missing hint: $hint\"\n        fi\n    done\n\n    if [ $hints_found -ge 4 ]; then\n        log_success \"Footer shows sufficient hotkey hints ($hints_found/${#hints[@]})\"\n    else\n        log_fail \"Footer missing too many hotkey hints ($hints_found/${#hints[@]})\"\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Step 4: Cleanup\n    log_info \"Step 4: Cleaning up...\"\n    stop_forge \"$session\"\n\n    return $result\n}\n\n# ==============================================================================\n# Main\n# ==============================================================================\n\nmain() {\n    echo \"\"\n    echo \"========================================\"\n    echo \"FORGE View Navigation Test\"\n    echo \"========================================\"\n\n    # Adjust for CI environment\n    ci_adjust\n\n    local start_time=$SECONDS\n    local result=0\n    local tests_passed=0\n    local tests_total=4\n\n    # Run view hotkey tests\n    echo \"\"\n    if test_view_hotkeys; then\n        ((tests_passed++)) || true\n    else\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Run tab navigation tests\n    echo \"\"\n    if test_tab_navigation; then\n        ((tests_passed++)) || true\n    else\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Run help overlay tests\n    echo \"\"\n    if test_help_overlay; then\n        ((tests_passed++)) || true\n    else\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Run footer hotkey tests\n    echo \"\"\n    if test_footer_hotkeys; then\n        ((tests_passed++)) || true\n    else\n        result=1\n    fi\n\n    # Summary\n    local elapsed=$((SECONDS - start_time))\n    echo \"\"\n    if [ $result -eq 0 ];
